10: defmodule MyApp.Server do
11:   @moduledoc """
12:   GenServer implementation for managing application state.
14:   Handles synchronous calls, asynchronous casts, and info messages.
15:   Supports state initialization with validated options.
16:   """
18:   use GenServer
19:   import Enum
20:   alias MyApp.Config
21:   alias MyApp.Server.State
22:   require Logger
24:   @behaviour GenServer
29:   @default_timeout 5_000
32:   @max_items 1_000
36:   @doc """
37:   Internal state struct for the server.
39:   @field name Server instance name.
40:   @field port Listening port number.
41:   @field items List of stored items.
42:   @field started_at Timestamp of server start.
43:   """
44:   defstruct [:name, :port, items: [], started_at: nil]
48:   @type t :: %__MODULE__{
49:     name: String.t(),
50:     port: non_neg_integer(),
51:     items: list(any()),
52:     started_at: DateTime.t() | nil
53:   }
55:   @type option :: {:name, String.t()} | {:port, non_neg_integer()}
59:   @doc """
60:   Start a new server process linked to the caller.
62:   @param opts Keyword list of options (:name, :port).
63:   @return {:ok, pid} on success or {:error, reason} on failure.
64:   """
65:   @spec start_link(keyword()) :: GenServer.on_start()
66:   def start_link(opts) do
67:     name = Keyword.get(opts, :name, "default")
68:     GenServer.start_link(__MODULE__, opts, name: via_tuple(name))
69:   end
71:   @doc """
72:   Add an item to the server's collection.
74:   @param server The server PID or registered name.
75:   @param item The item to store.
76:   @return :ok
77:   """
78:   @spec add_item(GenServer.server(), any()) :: :ok
79:   def add_item(server, item) do
80:     GenServer.call(server, {:add_item, item})
81:   end
83:   @doc """
84:   Retrieve all stored items.
86:   @param server The server PID or registered name.
87:   @return List of stored items.
88:   """
89:   @spec get_items(GenServer.server()) :: list(any())
90:   def get_items(server) do
91:     GenServer.call(server, :get_items)
92:   end
94:   @doc """
95:   Asynchronously notify the server of an event.
97:   @param server The server PID.
98:   @param event The event data to process.
99:   @return :ok
100:   """
101:   @spec notify(GenServer.server(), any()) :: :ok
102:   def notify(server, event) do
103:     GenServer.cast(server, {:notify, event})
104:   end
111:   defp via_tuple(name) do
112:     {:via, Registry, {MyApp.Registry, name}}
113:   end
117:   @impl true
118:   def init(opts) do
120:     name = Keyword.fetch!(opts, :name)
121:     port = Keyword.get(opts, :port, 4000)
123:     state = %__MODULE__{
124:       name: name,
125:       port: port,
126:       started_at: DateTime.utc_now()
127:     }
129:     Logger.info("Server #{name} started on port #{port}")
130:     {:ok, state}
131:   end
133:   @impl true
134:   def handle_call({:add_item, item}, _from, state) do
136:     if length(state.items) >= @max_items do
137:       {:reply, {:error, :capacity_exceeded}, state}
138:     else
139:       new_state = %{state | items: [item | state.items]}
140:       {:reply, :ok, new_state}
141:     end
142:   end
144:   @impl true
145:   def handle_call(:get_items, _from, state) do
146:     {:reply, Enum.reverse(state.items), state}
147:   end
149:   @impl true
150:   def handle_cast({:notify, event}, state) do
151:     Logger.debug("Received event: #{inspect(event)}")
152:     {:noreply, state}
153:   end
155:   @impl true
156:   def handle_info(:timeout, state) do
157:     Logger.warn("Server #{state.name} timed out")
158:     {:noreply, state}
159:   end
164:   defp handle_internal(state) do
166:     pruned = Enum.take(state.items, @max_items)
167:     %{state | items: pruned}
168:   end

171:   Define a macro for compile-time code generation.

175:   """
176:   defmacro my_macro(expr) do
177:     quote do

179:       Logger.debug("Macro result: #{inspect(result)}")
180:       result
181:     end
182:   end
183: end

224: defmodule MyApp.Utils do
225:   @moduledoc """
226:   Utility functions demonstrating pattern matching, guards,
227:   pipe operators, and comprehensions.
228:   """
230:   @doc """
231:   Classify a value using guard clauses and pattern matching.
233:   @param value The value to classify.
234:   @return Atom describing the value's category.
235:   """
236:   @spec classify(any()) :: atom()
237:   def classify(value) when is_integer(value) and value > 0, do: :positive
238:   def classify(value) when is_integer(value) and value < 0, do: :negative
239:   def classify(0), do: :zero
240:   def classify(value) when is_binary(value), do: :string
241:   def classify(value) when is_list(value), do: :list
242:   def classify(_), do: :unknown
244:   @doc """
245:   Process a list using pipe operator for data transformation.
247:   @param items List of strings to process.
248:   @return Sorted list of unique uppercase strings.
249:   """
250:   @spec process_list(list(String.t())) :: list(String.t())
251:   def process_list(items) do
252:     items
253:     |> Enum.map(&String.trim/1)
254:     |> Enum.reject(&(&1 == ""))
255:     |> Enum.map(&String.upcase/1)
256:     |> Enum.uniq()
257:     |> Enum.sort()
258:   end
260:   @doc """
261:   Demonstrate for-comprehension with multiple generators and filters.
263:   @param range Upper bound for the search space.
264:   @return List of Pythagorean triples.
265:   """
266:   @spec pythagorean_triples(pos_integer()) :: list(tuple())
267:   def pythagorean_triples(range) do
268:     for a <- 1..range,
269:         b <- a..range,
270:         c <- b..range,
271:         a * a + b * b == c * c do
272:       {a, b, c}
273:     end
274:   end
276:   @doc """
277:   Safe division returning :error tuple on zero divisor.
279:   @param a Dividend.
280:   @param b Divisor.
281:   @return {:ok, result} or {:error, :division_by_zero}.
282:   """
283:   @spec safe_divide(number(), number()) :: {:ok, float()} | {:error, atom()}
284:   def safe_divide(_, 0), do: {:error, :division_by_zero}
285:   def safe_divide(a, b), do: {:ok, a / b}
287:   @doc """
288:   Recursively compute factorial with guard clause.
290:   @param n Non-negative integer.
291:   @return Factorial of n.
292:   """
293:   @spec factorial(non_neg_integer()) :: pos_integer()
294:   def factorial(0), do: 1
295:   def factorial(n) when n > 0, do: n * factorial(n - 1)
296: end
